products.name: The Sorbet
products.description: Discover the art of understated affection with this blend of delicate pink and creamy white roses. We like to think it offers a beautifully subtle way to send your love. Perfect for conveying deep emotions with a soft, elegant touch. We're committed to delivering bouquets that match our photos. Occasionally, we might replace certain flowers due to seasonal availability, but your bouquet's overall appearance will remain unchanged.
products.care_instructions:
1) Find a Vase: You may have noticed your flowers no longer have roots, and that they’re not in soil. Bet they’re thirsty! Time to put them in your favorite vase with some fresh water.

2) Trim Those Stems: Keep those stems trimmed! Always cut your flowers at an angle so they can absorb more water & won’t suffocate on the bottom of the vase. After the initial cut, check the bottoms of your stems every couple of days, and if the ends are looking a bit slimy, just snip off an inch or so.

3) Remove Leaves: Take off any excess leaves so that all the water is funneled into the petals. Pay special attention to leaves below the water line because they’ll decompose, causing rot and bacteria to grow, which will shorten the lifespan of your blooms.

4) Remove Spent Blooms: Any flowers that are past their prime release ethylene gas which will age the flowers around them. Remove any flowers that are beginning to wilt to keep the rest of your bouquet fresh and happy.

5) Keep Them Cool: Last but not least, keep your flowers in a cool area. Don’t put them near radiators, computers, televisions, fireplaces, ovens… you get the idea.

products.name: The Peony
products.description:
Lush and full of life, it's not every day that you'll come across these pillowy stems. This striking blush-toned bouquet is a welcome addition to any room. Peonies have become symbolic with prosperity, making this a fitting gift for anything from congratulations to housewarmings. This peony arrangement may arrive in bud form but will unfurl into the pillowy stem we all love. Read more about the meaning of the pale pink peony. Choose from three bouquet sizes of lush peonies, a 10-stem Single, a 20-stem Double, or a 30-stem Triple.

Please note: Your peonies may arrive in bud form but will open in 1-2 days, meaning you can enjoy your blooms longer! Flower color may vary slightly from the pictured bouquet and may gradually change as your flowers bloom.
If your foliage looks slightly dehydrated upon arrival, place the stems in some water to revive them. (Pro tip: remove any foliage near or below the waterline for a longer-lasting bouquet!)

Allergens: Contains dairy, eggs, soy, wheat, peanuts, tree nuts (walnuts).

products.care_instructions:
1) Find a Vase: You may have noticed your flowers no longer have roots, and that they’re not in soil. Bet they’re thirsty! Time to put them in your favorite vase with some fresh water.

2) Trim Those Stems: Keep those stems trimmed! Always cut your flowers at an angle so they can absorb more water & won’t suffocate on the bottom of the vase. After the initial cut, check the bottoms of your stems every couple of days, and if the ends are looking a bit slimy, just snip off an inch or so.

3) Remove Leaves: Take off any excess leaves so that all the water is funneled into the petals. Pay special attention to leaves below the water line because they’ll decompose, causing rot and bacteria to grow, which will shorten the lifespan of your blooms.

4) Remove Spent Blooms: Any flowers that are past their prime release ethylene gas which will age the flowers around them. Remove any flowers that are beginning to wilt to keep the rest of your bouquet fresh and happy.

5) Keep Them Cool: Last but not least, keep your flowers in a cool area. Don’t put them near radiators, computers, televisions, fireplaces, ovens… you get the idea.

2) Trim Those Stems: Keep those stems trimmed! Always cut your flowers at an angle so they can absorb more water & won’t suffocate on the bottom of the vase. After the initial cut, check the bottoms of your stems every couple of days, and if the ends are looking a bit slimy, just snip off an inch or so.

3) Remove Leaves: Take off any excess leaves so that all the water is funneled into the petals. Pay special attention to leaves below the water line because they’ll decompose, causing rot and bacteria to grow, which will shorten the lifespan of your blooms.

4) Remove Spent Blooms: Any flowers that are past their prime release ethylene gas which will age the flowers around them. Remove any flowers that are beginning to wilt to keep the rest of your bouquet fresh and happy.

5) Keep Them Cool: Last but not least, keep your flowers in a cool area. Don’t put them near radiators, computers, televisions, fireplaces, ovens… you get the idea.

It’s arranged in a hand-blown Moroccan glass pitcher made from recycled beer and wine bottles, giving discarded glass a refined second life. The transparent blue hue, organic shape, and natural bubbles make the vessel a standout, adding color, culture, and versatility to any table long after the flowers fade.
products.care_instructions:
1) Find a Vase: You may have noticed your flowers no longer have roots, and that they’re not in soil. Bet they’re thirsty! Time to put them in your favorite vase with some fresh water.

2) Trim Those Stems: Keep those stems trimmed! Always cut your flowers at an angle so they can absorb more water & won’t suffocate on the bottom of the vase. After the initial cut, check the bottoms of your stems every couple of days, and if the ends are looking a bit slimy, just snip off an inch or so.

3) Remove Leaves: Take off any excess leaves so that all the water is funneled into the petals. Pay special attention to leaves below the water line because they’ll decompose, causing rot and bacteria to grow, which will shorten the lifespan of your blooms.

4) Remove Spent Blooms: Any flowers that are past their prime release ethylene gas which will age the flowers around them. Remove any flowers that are beginning to wilt to keep the rest of your bouquet fresh and happy.

5) Keep Them Cool: Last but not least, keep your flowers in a cool area. Don’t put them near radiators, computers, televisions, fireplaces, ovens… you get the idea.

products.name: The Neutral Orchids
products.description: Sometimes the most meaningful gestures are the simplest ones—The Neutral Orchids features multiple white orchids arranged in a clean white ceramic boat. Our designer created this serene arrangement for those quiet moments when you want to say "I'm here for you" without saying a word. Perfect for offering comfort during difficult times or celebrating someone who appreciates understated elegance. Beauty that speaks softly but says everything.
products.care_instructions:
Light: Orchids prefer bright, indirect light. Place your orchid about 1 - 3 feet back from a South or West facing window to ensure the plant gets bright light but doesn’t get burned by the direct sun.

Water: Water your orchid with 3 ounces or 2 ice cubes of water every week. In most cases, this will work. Occasionally, you’ll want to check the dryness of the potting medium (the moss the orchid is planted in). If it’s very dry, give the plant a more thorough watering.

Note: ALWAYS water below the leaves. If water flows into and sits and the center of the leaves, it will lead to crown rot and kill the orchid. If water does get between the leaves, soak it up with cotton buds or tissue. You can also blow gently into the meeting of the leaves to remove this water. Notes & Tips: Keep me warm! Orchids are very sensitive to changes in temperature. If your orchid is exposed to cold (under 55 degrees) for more than 10 minutes, the unbloomed buds will begin to “blast” – turn yellow and shrivel up, and eventually fall off the plant. There’s no reversing this once it’s happened. Consider sending fresh flowers or another plant during the winter months.

products.name: The Matcha
products.description: A living accent that feels like a sculptural art piece. This easy-to-care-for pothos plant is housed in a green-and-white marbled clay planter with layered ring detailing and a painted green base. Each vessel features natural variation, giving it depth and character. Modern, tactile, and designed to elevate everyday greenery.
products.care_instructions:
Light: This plant needs moderate to bright light to thrive, so place it near a southern or western facing window for best results. In brighter light, this plant will have golden variegation (stripes) on its leaves, which will fade in dimmer conditions.

Water: Water this plant when the top 2 - 3 inches of soil are completely dry to the touch. Pothos’ roots will decay in very wet soil, so be sure to test for dryness before giving it a drink. If the leaves begin to drop, it’s time for a good drink.

Notes & Tips: Pothos is sometimes called Devil’s Ivy because it’s so hardy! With proper care, this plant can live for many years.

Pet Safety: Pothos plants are not pet-safe. Place on a high table or shelf away from furry companions.

If water does get between the leaves, soak it up with cotton buds or tissue. You can also blow gently into the meeting of the leaves to remove this water. Notes & Tips: Keep me warm! Orchids are very sensitive to changes in temperature. If your orchid is exposed to cold (under 55 degrees) for more than 10 minutes, the unbloomed buds will begin to “blast” – turn yellow and shrivel up, and eventually fall off the plant. There’s no reversing this once it’s happened. Consider sending fresh flowers or another plant during the winter months.

products.name: The Serenity
products.description: Known for its serene white blooms and deep green leaves, this plant serves as a graceful addition to any interior space. Its understated beauty makes it an especially thoughtful choice for sympathy sends, offering a lasting tribute of comfort and peace. Ideal for both gifting and personal enjoyment, this Peace Lily is more than a houseplant—it's a gesture of heartfelt care.
products.care_instructions:
Light: Peace lilies tolerate light levels from low light to bright, filtered light. In general, your plant will grow more quickly and push out more flowers in brighter light. Avoid direct sunlight, which can burn and blanche the leaves. Eastern and southern exposures are the vibe.

Water: Water your plant when the top 2-3 inches of soil are dry to the touch. Peace Lilies are very slow drinkers, and you’ll probably find that you’re watering your plant every 8 - 12 days, depending on the light and warmth in your home. Peace Lily leaves will begin to droop if the plant becomes very dry but will pop right back up after a drink. Don’t let this happen too often tho, as it stresses the plant.

Notes & Tips: The white “flowers” that we’re used to seeing on Peace Lilies aren’t flowers at all. They’re called a spadix, which is a structure that protects the actual flower – the prickly little thing inside the white spadix.

Pet Safety: Philodendron and all aroid plants are not pet-safe. Place on a high table or shelf away from furry companions.

products.name: The Rosy Dawn
products.description: A vibrant yet refined houseplant designed to bring color and life indoors. The calathea plant features striking green foliage with pink undersides and is known as a “prayer plant,” gently moving its leaves in response to light throughout the day. Styled in the deep pink matte ceramic vessel with a rounded, fluted silhouette, it feels modern yet timeless. A thoughtful, expressive plant that elevates everyday spaces.
products.care_instructions:
Light: Calathea thrive in moderate to bright light, but do not want direct sun exposure. In a bright spot, your plant will have vibrant multi-color leaves with stripes of pink and white. In a dimmer spot, the color will be more subtle.

Water: Water when the top 2 inches of soil are nearly dry to the touch. In general, this will be about every 4 - 8 days depending on the humidity, light, and temperature. They also need high humidity levels of at least 55 %, otherwise the leaves can get brown tips and may start to shrivel up. Do not place your triostar calathea next to a radiator, as the air there becomes very dry, especially in winter. Mist 1-2 times weekly during especially dry periods.

Pro-Tip: Warm dry enviroments make all kinds of plants (Calathea, Ivy, and others) especially succeptible to spider mites, a common houseplant pest. In general, it's best practice to keep a humidifier near your houseplants for best success.

products.name: The Seaside
products.description: A fresh, coastal-inspired plant that captures the calm of spring by the water. Lush purple hydrangea blooms are paired with a soft light blue vessel, echoing sea and sky tones. Hydrangeas are traditionally associated with gratitude and heartfelt emotion, adding quiet meaning to the design. Cool, serene, and effortlessly refined.
products.care_instructions:
Light: Ornamental Hydrangea need bright light to encourage their blooms. Place in a Southern facing window or in a spot getting several hours of bright sun every day. Avoid Western facing windows, which can get too hot for this plant. 

Water: Hydrangea tend to be thirsty plants, so it's important to check the soil for moisture every day. If the top inch of soil feels nearly dry to the touch, that's when it's time to water. If the flowers ever seem to be drooping, that could also be a sign that your hydrangea needs water. 

Fun facts: These hydrangea were grown in greenhouses but, depending on your USDA zone (your best shot at success is in zones 5-9), you may be able to plant yours outside once it's done blooming and get it to naturalize in the garden. Once the bloom has fallen, plant in a spot in the garden facing south east, so the plant gets morning sunlight and afternoon shade.
